titleStmt.title: SII 20.75: original edition by G. S. Gai
No. 75. (B.K. No. 49 of 1936-37.) KALKĒRI, SINDGI TALUK, BIJAPUR DISTRICT.

p.hi: Slab set up in a ruined temple in Survey No. 6.

summary.p: This record is dated in the cyclic year Hēmaḷambi, Vaiśākha, śu. 5. Sunday, in the reign of the king. This corresponds to A.D. 1117, April 8, Sunday, f.d.t. ·07.

The inscription states that Amṛita-Paṭṭavarddhana-Vāsudēva-Ghaḷisāsa and his wife Chāmiyakka consecrated the image of Kēśavadēva with the consent of the Mahājanas of Kalkere which had been made into a sarvvanamasya-agrahāra by Mahāpradhāna, Daṇḍanāyaka Kāḷimarasa and endowed a gift of land to the same.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
© śrī śrī śrī śrī śrī śrī śrī
svasti samastabhuvanāśraya śrī prithvīvallabhaṁ mahā
rājādhirājaṁ paramēśvara paramabhaṭṭārakaṁ satyā
śrayakuḷatiḷakaṁ cāḷukyābharaṇaṁ śrīmat tribhuva
namalladēvara vijayarājyamuttarōttarābhivṛddhi
pravarddhamānamācaṁdrārkkatāraṁbaraṁ salutta
mire kalyāṇapurada nelevīḍinoḷu sukhasankathāvinōdadiṁ
rājyangeyyattamire . svasti samadhigatapaṁcamahāśabda mahāsaṁ
ndhivigrahādhipati mahāpracaṁḍḍadaṁḍanayaka vairibhayadāyakaṁ sāhityavidyā
brihaspati guṇa payaśvapati sāha
satyarādhēya vākpatikarṇāvataṁsaṁ kaṁṭhīrava
rava rigara samuddharaṇanayyanagandhavāraṇa
nāmādi samastaprasastisahitaṁ śrīmanmahāpradhānaṁ kaḍitaverggaḍe kannaḍasaṁdhivigra
hi daṇḍanāyaka kāḷimarasaru . biṭṭa sarvvanamasyadagrahāra kalkeṟeya A
śēsamahājanaṁgaḷa Anumatiyiṁ hēmaḷaṁbi saṁvatsarada vaiśā
kha suddha paṁcami Ādivāradaṁdu svasti yamaniyamasvādhyā
yadhyānadhāraṇamōnānuṣṭhāna japasamādhisīlagu
pannarappa Amrita paṭṭavardhana vāsudēva ghaḷisāsaru Avara madava
ḷige cāmiyakkanuṁ śrī kēśavadēvara pratiṣṭheyaṁ mā
ḍi biṭṭa bhūmi samasta baḷi sahitaṁ biṭṭa vritti Eraḍu I dha
rmakke Ārānu Aniṣṭaṁ māḍuvargge . . svadattaṁ paradattaṁ vā
yō harētu vasuṁdharā śaṣṭirvvarśasahaśrāṇā viṣṭā
yāṁ jāyatē krimi .
Digital edition of SII 20.75 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
